genre_refs: 'Left Exit 12 Off Ramp' / car drifting at highway-exit reaction meme
description: A designed meme built on the well-known 'Left Exit 12 Off Ramp' internet meme template: a still of a dark sedan veering sharply off a highway onto a last-second exit ramp beneath an overpass, smoke coming from its tires. The green overhead highway sign is edited so the straight-ahead arrow is labeled 'FUNDING GENDER EQUITY IN THE MEXICAN WORKFORCE' and the exit arrow is labeled 'INVESTING IN AMERICAN WORKERS'; the small 'LEFT EXIT 12' marker is retained at top. The official U.S. Department of Labor seal (blue circle, eagle and shield, 'DEPARTMENT OF LABOR / UNITED STATES OF AMERICA') is superimposed in the center over the swerving car. The meme uses the template's connotation of an abrupt, reckless choice to mock prior spending on foreign (Mexican) workforce gender-equity programs while signaling a swerve toward 'America First' domestic worker investment. This is an agency adopting a viral meme format for political contrast/ridicule. Target audience: online/younger social-media users and the political base.
notable_text: FUNDING GENDER EQUITY IN THE MEXICAN WORKFORCE | INVESTING IN AMERICAN WORKERS | LEFT EXIT 12 | DEPARTMENT OF LABOR UNITED STATES OF AMERICA
